Hey, Jim Launchers, you probably just hit publish on your ads, and it's about time that we start to get to know our markets. We set up an ad optimization call with you right after you hit publish, so I want to go over the things that you'll probably be looking over with your implementation tech. The first thing I'm going to do is show you the anatomy of an ad, just so you know kind of what I'm referencing. The page name is going to be this part up here at the top, so that's going to be the identity of your page under the ad level. The copy is probably something you're pretty familiar with at this time, so that's going to be the written text inside of your ad. Creative is going to be either your image or video. Call to action is going to be this button right here. Your headline is going to be the part written in bold, and the description is going to be the line right underneath it. So most of what we'll be going over today is in this lead gin scrambler video, or you can get ideas for this from the lead gin scrambler video. So we have our headline copy, etc., etc. So let's go over this know your market sheet. The first thing you're going to be doing is putting in the page name, so the identity that you're using, because you can have more than one and you can experiment with those. The next thing is going to be the campaign type. So let's figure out what works in your market best. Is it the 42-day detox? Is it the six-week challenge, 12-week HTTP? And are you doing this challenge hybrid or in-gym? The next thing in the column is going to be your CPR, so that's your cost per result. If you have any trouble finding that, the implementation text can help you find that. So for example, I've put in two examples for you. You can see that one of these is at 967, the other is at 754. How long has the ad been running is going to go right here. The gender, which is located in the ad set level in the age range, are going to go right here so we can figure out what age range is and what genders are working best. The headline, the description, the call to action. So you have a few different options in here that we suggest a little bit more so than others. The first two that we suggest the most are going to be apply now or book now. Everything underneath are other things that you can try out, but these are definitely going to be the two strongest. After that is going to be the creative. So be really descriptive. That way you know really well what works in your market. So for example, maybe you had a black and white video of a blonde female doing medball slams or a colorful meal prep image that looks very professional like this one. The next part that we have is a variable that we can experiment with is the copy. So in here, start out with is the copy long or short? Is it free or non free? And then if you feel so inclined, you can put the entire, you can just paste the whole copy in here or you can link it that way. It doesn't take up the whole sheet. So if you're looking for ideas of things to experiment, I just want to shout out one more time that the Legion scrambler video is going to give you a lot of different variables and a lot of different ideas of things that you can do. So in case you haven't seen it, here's that video and we look forward to meeting with you and talking about ways that you can better optimize your ads.
